title: Walden
author: Henry David Thoreau
content: “Our life is frittered away by detail. Simplify, simplify.”
action: Cut one non-essential layer from your current process.

title: The Analects
author: Confucius
content: “It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.”
action: Maintain your current momentum; ignore the rate of progress.

title: Tao Te Ching
author: Lao Tzu
content: “He who knows that enough is enough will always have enough.”
action: Define the threshold where 'good enough' becomes 'done'.

title: Critique of Pure Reason
author: Immanuel Kant
content: “Thoughts without content are empty, intuitions without concepts are blind.”
action: Verify your strategy is supported by hard, factual data.

title: Essays
author: Michel de Montaigne
content: “The greatest thing in the world is to know how to belong to oneself.”
action: Assert full ownership of your decision-making.
